sessions.description: Welcome to DSM 2026. This session introduces the program structure, cohort members, faculty, and what to expect over the coming months.
sessions.bullet_1: Who should attend: All accepted DSM 2026 participants
sessions.bullet_2: Key takeaway: Program roadmap, expectations, and how to get the most from DSM
sessions.bullet_3: Prerequisite: None — this is the first session

sessions.description: An introduction to deploying AI agents in real-world environments, covering architecture patterns, reliability, and observability.
sessions.bullet_1: Who should attend: All DSM 2026 track participants
sessions.bullet_2: Key takeaway: Agent deployment patterns and common failure modes
sessions.bullet_3: Prerequisite: Completion of Module 2 reading materials

sessions.description: How to build robust evaluation pipelines for large language model applications, from unit evals to production monitoring.
sessions.bullet_1: Who should attend: Engineers and product leads working with LLMs
sessions.bullet_2: Key takeaway: Building automated eval harnesses for LLM apps
sessions.bullet_3: Prerequisite: Familiarity with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, or similar)

sessions.description: Designing systems where multiple AI agents collaborate on complex tasks. Covers coordination patterns, message passing, and failure handling.
sessions.bullet_1: Who should attend: All participants with Module 3 complete
sessions.bullet_2: Key takeaway: Orchestration patterns for reliable multi-agent pipelines
sessions.bullet_3: Prerequisite: Session 1 (AI Agents in Production) or equivalent experience
